competitor_patterns: Extreme depth: Bikerumor's #1-ranking post is 24,884 words — it functions as a living buyer's guide updated repeatedly (last May 2026), covering 18+ tested products with individual mini-reviews, comparison tables, and FAQs. Our longest post is 4,045 words.

Freshness signals matter: every top-3 competitor explicitly dates their content (e.g. 'May 21, 2026', 'May 19, 2026') and uses phrases like 'updated for summer 2026' or 'Editor's note' to signal recency — Google rewards this for seasonal buying-guide queries.

Structured content elements dominate: all top-3 English results combine H2-level individual product reviews, a comparison table, FAQ schema, and 'how we test' credibility sections — no single element is optional at that ranking level.

Interchangeable-lens positioning is a category keyword: DHgate ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses', showing transactional intent around this specific feature — competitors frame it as a purchase decision driver, not just a feature mention.

Competitor roundups include budget AND premium tiers side-by-side: 220Triathlon targets 'best cheap cycling glasses' at position 3 with a table, showing that price-segmented framing (budget vs premium) reliably earns featured snippets and top slots.
our_gaps: Word count ceiling: our best pages cap at ~4,000 words while the #1 competitor for our most-clicked queries runs 24,884 words. We lack the depth signals Google uses to assess topical authority — we need at least one pillar post at 8,000–12,000 words for 'best cycling glasses 2026' cluster.

Missing 'how we test' credibility section: no Velluto post includes a methodology or testing-criteria section. Bikerumor and Cyclist both lead with this — it builds E-E-A-T signals and is cited by Google AI Overviews as a trust marker.

FAQ schema coverage is only 33%: 27 of our ~40 posts lack FAQ schema. GSC shows near-top positions (6–8) for high-impression queries like 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (260 imp) — FAQ schema is a direct lever for featured snippet capture at those positions.

No 'updated for 2026' freshness signals in titles or meta descriptions: competitors explicitly label update dates in their snippets. Our meta descriptions and titles are static, reducing CTR from searchers who filter by recency.

Thin content cluster (1,000–1,350 words) is too large: 28 of our posts are under 1,400 words. These pages earn impressions but convert poorly to clicks (see: 'veluto' at 0.9% CTR despite pos 5.7). They lack the content signals to push past page 1 mid-rankings.
keyword_opportunities: 'lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30g' — no competitor content precisely targets sub-30g weight as a primary buying criterion; StradaPro at 25g is a perfect anchor and no current Velluto post leads with this exact spec-driven query.

'interchangeable lens cycling glasses tool-free' — DHgate owns 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' at #1 but with no quality content; a tool-free/click-in lens angle is uncontested by editorial sites and maps directly to VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione USP.

'cycling glasses 30 day trial' or 'try before you buy cycling glasses' — zero editorial competitor content targets the risk-free trial angle as a primary search query; this is a conversion-intent keyword that Velluto could own entirely.

'anti-fog cycling glasses hot weather 2026' — our anti-fog posts exist but are thin (1,032–1,153 words); Bikerumor's roundup mentions anti-fog as a feature but no competitor has a dedicated, deeply tested anti-fog hot-weather guide at adequate depth.

'best cycling glasses for climbers 2026' — Tour de France and climbing-specific content is trending (our post gets impressions); no competitor has a dedicated climbers' glasses guide targeting the weight + ventilation + lens-swap combination.
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 6,000 words for any post targeting 'best cycling glasses 2026', 'best cycling sunglasses 2026', or 'oakley alternatives' — these queries show 60–269 impressions with positions 6–8, meaning depth is the gap between us and top 3.

Open every post with a dateline and update signal in the first 50 words: e.g. 'Last tested: July 2026 — we rode X km with these glasses to update this guide.' Google surfaces this text in snippets and it directly lifts CTR for date-sensitive queries like 'best cycling glasses 2026'.

Include a 'How We Test' H2 section (150–200 words minimum) covering: ride conditions, km ridden, lens-swap tests, anti-fog validation, weight verification. This is the single most-cited E-E-A-T gap vs Bikerumor and Cyclist.

Every post targeting a 'best X' or comparison query must include: (1) a scannable comparison table near the top, (2) individual H2-level mini-reviews per product, (3) FAQ schema with 4+ questions. These three elements appear in 100% of top-3 results for our target queries.

For posts targeting 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.2, 269 imp, 10 clicks): lead with the price-vs-spec comparison angle — state the Oakley Sutro Lite price vs StradaPro price in the first paragraph with exact figures, and use a table showing weight, lens system, trial policy side-by-side.

Integrate the 30-day risk-free trial CTA as a recurring anchor throughout content — competitors have no equivalent offer, and this differentiator should appear in the intro, mid-content, and conclusion. Use it as a FAQ question ('Can I try Velluto before committing?') to capture zero-click featured snippet opportunities.

For thin posts under 1,400 words (28 pages): prioritise expanding the top 8 by GSC impressions first — add FAQ schema, a comparison table, and a 'Velluto vs [relevant competitor]' section to each. Do not publish new thin posts; expand existing ones.
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27 posts currently missing it — prioritise in this order based on impression volume: sigma-vs-velluto, best-cycling-glasses-for-tour-de-france-conditions-2026, best-cycling-glasses-for-all-weather-conditions-2026, interchangeable-lens-cycling-glasses-2026-best-systems-tested, roka-vs-velluto. Each FAQ should include 4–6 questions mapping to People Also Ask boxes for that query cluster.

Add a comparison table to all 20 posts currently missing one — minimum 4 columns: Product, Weight, Lens System, Price. Table presence correlates with top-3 ranking in 100% of competitor pages analysed; it also enables featured snippet table capture for comparison queries.

Rewrite all meta descriptions to include a freshness signal ('Tested July 2026'), a specific numeric differentiator ('25g, click-in lenses'), and a risk-reversal hook ('30-day trial') — this directly addresses the low CTR on 'veluto' (0.9% at pos 5.7) and 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (2.7% at pos 7.7).
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (2.7% CTR, pos 7.7) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Why: Adding 'Tested & Ranked' mirrors Bikerumor's #1 title format ('Bikerumor Tested') which sets an editorial credibility bar; the colon structure improves scannability in SERPs and the year anchors freshness for this date-sensitive query.

QUERY: 'veluto' (0.9% CTR, pos 5.7) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Site | StradaPro | Why: This is a misspelling of 'Velluto' indicating branded navigational intent — the user wants the official store. Adding 'Official Site' and the product name reassures the searcher they've found the right destination and will lift CTR from 0.9% significantly; include the correct spelling in the title to also capture the corrected search.
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'velluto' (pos 4.7, 1005 imp, 52 clicks) → Topic: Homepage/brand landing page optimisation | Add: Ensure the homepage H1 includes 'Velluto Cycling Glasses', add a 'Why Velluto' section with the 25g weight, 30-day trial, and lens system named explicitly; add FAQ schema to the homepage with brand-intent questions ('Is Velluto an Italian brand?', 'What is the StradaPro?') to push from pos 4.7 to top 3 and lift CTR above 5.2%.

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.2, 269 imp, 10 clicks) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: Lighter, Cheaper, Same Performance' | Add: Expand existing Oakley alternatives posts to 6,000+ words; add a top-of-page quick-verdict table comparing Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro on weight/price/lens system; add a 'Why cyclists switch from Oakley' H2 with 5 data-backed reasons; add FAQ schema with 'Is Velluto better than Oakley?' as the first question.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 6.7, 149 imp, 6 clicks) → Topic: Consolidate and redirect thin duplicate posts into best-cycling-glasses-2026-the-complete-buying-guide | Add: Merge word count to 7,000+ words; ensure table and FAQ schema are present (table=yes already); add a price-tier breakdown H2 ('Under €100', '€100–€200', 'Over €200') which is the format used by all top-3 competitors for this query.

QUERY: 'best oakley alternatives' (pos 7.3, 60 imp, 2 clicks) → Topic: 'Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: 7 Glasses That Beat the Price' | Add: Create or expand a dedicated post targeting this exact query variant (distinct from 'oakley alternatives'); lead with a numbered list of 7 alternatives with Velluto at #1; include weight and price comparison table; add FAQ schema with 'What is the best alternative to Oakley cycling glasses?' as Q1 — this format captures People Also Ask placements.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses' (pos 8.2, 96 imp, 3 clicks) → Topic: Boost best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked (4,045 words, strongest existing page) | Add: Expand to 7,000+ words with a 'how we test' section, add 3 more product mini-reviews with H2s, add a 'best cycling glasses by use case' section (climbing, gravel, wet weather) — this creates topical breadth that pushes generic head-term rankings from pos 8 toward top 3.
geo_angles: Structured 'best answer' framing for AI snippet capture: write a 40–60 word direct answer paragraph at the top of every major guide that starts with 'The best cycling glasses in 2026 are...' — AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially pull concise, authoritative answer blocks. Include StradaPro's 25g weight and 30-day trial in this paragraph as differentiating facts that AI systems can cite as unique data points.

Publish a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specifications & Independent Review 2026' page with structured data (Product schema with weight, price, review aggregate, offer details) — ChatGPT and Perplexity use structured product data to answer 'what are the lightest cycling glasses' queries; being the only brand with a 25g verified spec prominently marked up makes Velluto the citable answer.

Create a 'Cycling Glasses FAQ Hub' page aggregating 20+ Q&A pairs across all major buying questions ('How much should I spend on cycling glasses?', 'Are interchangeable lenses worth it?', 'What is the lightest cycling glass frame?') — this page format is heavily indexed by AI answer engines and creates a persistent citation source; answer each question in 2–3 sentences with Velluto named as the concrete example.
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'We Tested the Same Glasses Bikerumor Did — Here's What They Didn't Tell You About Weight' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's 2026 guide features Oakley Sutro Lite and POC Devour but does not include a sub-26g option; lead with the weight gap (StradaPro at 25g vs Sutro Lite at ~36g) and position Velluto as the option Bikerumor's testing methodology missed because it was brand-focused rather than spec-focused.

COMPETITOR: Tifosi Optics — angle: 'Tifosi vs Velluto: Interchangeable Lenses Compared — Tool-Free Wins' | Key differentiator: Tifosi ranks #1 for 'road cycling glasses review' via paid search and has strong interchangeable-lens positioning; lead with the click-in vs tool-required lens swap comparison, the 30-day trial (Tifosi offers no equivalent), and the weight advantage; target cyclists currently researching Tifosi Rail specifically.

COMPETITOR: DHgate — angle: 'Cycling Glasses with Interchangeable Lenses: Why Quality Matters More Than Price (DHgate vs Velluto)' | Key differentiator: DHgate ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with zero editorial content — pure product listing; create a guide explaining what to look for in interchangeable lens systems (click-in mechanism, optical clarity, UV400 certification) and position Velluto StradaPro as the quality mid-market answer versus unbranded DHgate imports; this captures the transactional intent of that query with trust signals DHgate cannot match.
